posts.text: Senator Mark Warner called the Iran a “war of choice”. His Senate Intelligence Committee has yet to receive any proof that there was imminent reason for the Pentagon to attack Iran.  And @teammarkwarner is right.  The decision created three set of problems: it replaced Iran’s leadership, it wasn’t authorized by Congress, and it’s costing millions of dollars an hour.

Donald Trump posted on social media a message to the Iranian soccer team, telling them that they should not come to the United States to play for the FIFA World Cup because their safety would be in danger.  After that message, the Iran team asked FIFA to move their game to Mexico, but FIFA rejected the request

After attacking Iran, Trump sets his sights on Cuba, telling the press that he can “do anything he wants” with Cuba. President Trump said that he can free Cuba or take it over. While “taking over” a sovereign nation is a violation of international law, this sentiment is the application of concepts that the president outlined in the “national security strategy“ document presented earlier this year to Congress.

The new talking point following the attacks by the US and Israel against Iran is that “Trump didn’t start that war. He ended a 47 year war.“ that is not the case for many reasons. Let’s fact check people like Vance Kay

Does Virginia House Bill 110 create a ban anywhere on Capitol Grounds but allows state lawmakers to avoid that ban? @The Older Millennial seems to think that, but that’s not at all what the bill says. The bill governs what needs to be locked in a car when someone parks on Capitol grounds. Let’s fact check him (again).

Why are the democrats refusing to fund DHS and TSA, causing huge lines at airport security? This isn’t about politics, but about common sense.  Democratic lawmakers are asking for common sense policies in immigration enforcement, to ensure the safety of everyone and the respect for the constitution.  Responding to @PowerforFlorida
Erika Kirk spoke about how she and her husband, Charlie, felt about negative feelings to others, in any area.

SJSU volleyball player Brooke Slusser is now famous for telling her story about living for months with a teammate, Blaire Fleming, who is trans.  But @brookeslusser04 seems to have a problem with Fleming’s existing, and not with her actions.  Is Brooke’s reaction prejudice and intolerance or a valid concern?

Does the President need authorization to continue the Iran conflict? Yes, but the US Congress is cowering: the House and Senate voted down a resolution this would have stopped the conflict but then didn’t vote on authorization.  This tells the world “Trump doesn’t need authorization from Congress, even when the constitution requires it”.

Why are people saying that it’s Trump’s fault that gas prices are so high, now that we’re in a conflict with Iran, when they used to say that a President doesn’t control gas prices, under Biden? Because there’s a difference between high prices caused by a spike in demand and high prices caused by a conflict started by the president people are blaming.  Responding to @Kol
